observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant restore the unreleased
checks at year-end and reverse the disbursements related to stale checks to
avoid their repeated inclusion in subsequent Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Treasurer issue written
notices to the payees of stale checks and assess whether these checks are
eligible for replacement in accordance with applicable regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant return to the General
Fund the unspent balance of the LDRRM-CO amounting to ₱64,289.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
1. Notify in writing the payees of the stale checks and require them to return the check or issuance of a new one or waive their credit claims in favor of the LGU; and
2. Prepare a report of the cancelled checks on the period of cancellation and on the replacement checks and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry on:
3. the stale checks based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer in order to restore the amount of cash previously recorded as disbursements; and
4. the credit memos on the amounts added by the bank in favor of the LGU to correct the understatements of the Cash in Bank account.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
➢ Identify the valid claimants in the current Accounts Payable and
prepare the list comprising the balance, supported with the
Disbursement Vouchers and pertinent documents, to validate the
recorded payables;
➢ Revert to the Government Equity account those payables without
valid claimants, including the ₱40,807.35 in the Special Education
Fund which have remained in the books for more than two years;
furnish the Municipal Budget Officer with a copy of the Journal Entry
Voucher on the reversion; and maintain a record of the reverted A/Ps;
and
➢ Set an official deadline on the submission of claims at yearend and
accept for processing only the claims which are supported with
complete and necessary documents.

2.18 We recommended that:
a.) The Municipal Assessor:
➢ initiate and fast-track the updating of the Municipality’s assessment
roll through frequent physical survey and cleansing of records;
➢ prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer, on or before the
thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an Assessment Roll
containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been
newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties;

b.) The Municipal Treasurer:
➢ Maintain the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) based on the
report of the Municipal Assessor;
➢ Prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the RPTAR with the
amount due and collectible for the year, and submit the same to the
Accountant at the start of every year; and
➢ Conduct intensive tax campaign and information drive, in addition to
the posting of notices, to encourage more property owners to pay
taxes.

c.) The Municipal Accountant:
➢ record the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on
the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer;
➢ reconcile and correct the variances noted in the RPT/SET Receivable
General Ledgers.

2.19 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor impose the legal remedies
and exercise the taxing authority of the LGU as mandated in the Local
Government Code of 1991.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Municipal
Accountant, within five days after the end of December, the transaction
documents that occurred on the said month for immediate recording in the
books and avoid delay in the submission of the LGU’s financial statements.

disbursements on the year that the transaction occurred for accuracy and fair
presentation of balances in the financial statement.

a. return of the unused funds or remaining balances and forward the same
to the Municipal Treasurer for issuance of checks and remittance to the

appropriate income and Trust Liabilities – LDRRMF accounts, and
furnished the Budget Officer with the Journal Entry Voucher.

Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer:

year;
b.) Realign for other implementation-ready projects the funds of projects
that still have unresolved issues and provide a budget thereto only when
all required documents are ready for immediate implementation; and
c.) Implement during the budget year all the identified projects in the
Annual Investment Plan that are to be funded from the 20%
Development Fund.

b.) Summarize in the Report on Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) all
the RIS, on subsequent procurement of supplies, and submit the same to
the Office of the Municipal Accountant on a monthly basis to facilitate
the preparation of JEV to decrease the balance of the Inventory account.
3.9 Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record goods
purchased intended for distribution in the appropriate Inventory account
and reduce the account only when distribution is duly supported with the
required documents submitted monthly by the personnel who has custody of
these items.

4.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to:
a.) the Municipal DRRM Officer and the Municipal SWD Officer, to submit
to the Municipal Accountant the RIS and Distribution List of the 3,021
food packs, if these were distributed to the household beneficiaries, or the
Physical Inventory Report, if the food packs are still for distribution and
on hand;
b.) the Municipal Health Officer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the
Drug Utilization/Stocks Inventory Report as December 31, 2021 duly
supported with the RIS and Distribution List of the drugs and medicines
and medical supplies received and dispensed by the said office during the
year, as basis of the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the balances
appearing in the Inventory accounts.
4.7 Lastly, we recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical
count of the inventory items every end of the year and submit the RPCI to the
Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the balances in the Supplies
Ledger Card.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer provide the Municipal
Accountant with the complete documents relative to the vaccines received
from the DOH, as well as the doses administered at the end of each month.

perpetual inventory system, the vaccines received by the LGU and the doses
dispensed, using as reference the report/documents submitted by the MHO.

6.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Municipal
Assessor to comply the lacking documentary requirements and resolve the
deficiencies on the other documents, in compliance with Item 13.1 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 and submit to the COA Audit Team immediately to
avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
6.9 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer, assist the Municipal
Assessor in the completion of the lacking documentary requirements, and
desist from processing payments for future transactions, especially those
involving large amounts, unless the claim is supported with complete and
necessary documents.

Council, through the MDRRM Officer, exclude in the LDRRM Plan in the
ensuing years the allocation for activities which do not primarily address the
thematic areas of DRRM activities.
7.10 We also, recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize the utilization of the
LDRRMF only for disaster risk reduction and management activities to
avoid audit disallowances.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Heads of Offices,
who were designated incharge with the implementation of the programs
funded by the Department of Agriculture (DA), DENR, DOH and DILG, to
submit a certification to the Municipal Accountant that there are no on-going

₱1,859,780.17.

9.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant
stop procurements through reimbursement basis, and instead, comply
strictly with the provisions of RA No. 9184, or the Procurement Law, to

to VI and VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015,

system operations, notwithstanding the same have not been collected yet.

accounting policy in the recording of income from the operation of AWASS.

with the Department of Agriculture RO VIII to come up with the best solution

billboards/signboards with complete information in the venue where the PPA

information on the posting of project signboard and/or public notice shall be
provided to COA within ten (10) days after the award of infrastructure project
or before the start of the program/activity.

Management Office (LDRRMO) be required to ensure the optimal use of the
LDRRM Funds by implementing the planned programs and activities under
the four thematic areas for Mitigation Fund, which are necessary for the
attainment of the goals and objectives of the LGU’s LDRRM Plan.

6.4 We recommended that Management -
a. Require the concerned officials to strictly enforce AWASS’s policies
on the collection of water services fees. Disconnection of water
services from delinquent concessionaires should be strictly imposed
and reconnection or re-application of water services should only be
granted upon full payment of the arrears including the applicable
interests and penalties. Avail of legal remedies, if necessary in the
collection of past due accounts.;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to formulate measures
that will improve the assessment of business taxes such as, but not
limited to:
• Requiring all business establishments that the statement of gross
sales or receipts for the preceding calendar year should be
supported with documents such as audited financial statements
and BIR business tax returns (VAT and Percentage Tax
Remittance); and
• Inspecting business establishments within the jurisdiction of the
LGU to ensure effective and efficient collection of business taxes.
Also, require the Local Finance Committee to conduct periodic review
of collection performance to ascertain that revenue targets are attained.

Treasurer be required to remit in full the taxes withheld on income payments
to BIR, as well as the employees’ and employer’s contributions and loan
amortizations to GSIS and Pag-IBIG Fund, within the period prescribed in

1997, and RA No. 9679 or HDMF Law of 2009.

under RA No. 3456 as amended by RA No. 4177, to assist in the achievement

affairs and functions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare a
lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each depreciable assets.
Likewise, create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the
physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. The Inventory Committee shall
prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE).

The Local Chief Executive committed that they will comply with the audit
recommendations and will submit the Report on Physical Inventory of Plant, Property
and Equipment by June 2018.

The lapsing schedule shall show the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly
depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of
depreciable properties. While the periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted
onwards after the initial count and the Inventory Committee should be responsible for the
submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be
completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly
accounted, disposed and recorded. Their reports shall be reconciled with the book
balances, and the differences, if any shall be properly accounted for.

We recommend that the officials and employees concerned be required to
immediately settle/refund in full the unliquidated travel advances or enforce the
withholding of their salaries or any money due them if necessary, pursuant to
Section 4.1.3, 5.1.3 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 97-002. In no case, shall additional
travel advances be granted unless the the previous cash advance is first settled as
required in Section 4.1.2 of the same Circular. Moreover, look into the merits or
necessity of travels prior to approval of travel orders to avoid the granting of travel
advances that may not be pursued later, and stop the practice of granting excessive
cash advances.

We recommend that Management review the hiring procedure and
personnel management of Job Orders and set a standard policy and guidelines
relative thereto in accordance with the regulations set forth in Section 76 and 77 of
RA 7160 and the CSC Resolution No. 020790. Also, we recommend that
Management enjoin the concern officials to submit copies of Job Order Contract or
Contract of Service to the Auditor for evaluation.

We recommend that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 10,
R.A 9184, Section 54(1) and Item 2(a) of Annex “H” and other applicable
provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation issued under GPPB
Resolution No. 3-2009 dated July 22, 2009 in the procurement of goods.

We recommend that concerned officials be enjoined to comply with the
regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to uphold good local
governance through transparency and accountability. Likewise, require the
Municipal Engineer to inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the
appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the
project site.

We recommend that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized in
implementing development projects indicated in the Annual Investment
Plan.Likewise, see to it that the Annual Investment Plan clearly specify the
programs, projects and activities (PPAs) that are to be funded out of the
development fund as provided for under Item 2 of DILG MC No. 96-263 dated
December 9, 1996.

We recommend that management prioritize and provide adequate funding
for the Construction of the Sanitary Landfill to hasten the closure of the controlled
dumpsite and to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the implementation of the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 specifically on the following: 1. Stop the granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees if their previous cash advances are not yet fully liquidated. 2. Require the officers and employees concerned to liquidate cash advances within thirty (30) days for local travels and sixty (60) if travel abroad after his return to his permanent official station as required under Section 16 of EO 248 as amended. Likewise, other cash advances must, as a rule, be liquidated within the prescribed periods depending upon the nature and purpose of the cash advance as stated in COA Circular No 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. 3. Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the head of agency or his duly authorized representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate his cash advance for travel and for other purposes. This is to preclude complaints arising from suspension of salaries due to non-liquidation of their cash advances. 4. Suspend the salaries and delete the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time cash advances are fully liquidated. 5. Institute appropriate action for those who have no salaries that can be withheld.
observations.reason: Unliquidated cash advances have not been fully settled, thus leaving a considerable or material outstanding balance as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of
accounts the grant of financial assistance to Due from LGUs account and she should
maintain subsidiary records to properly monitor the liquidation of fund transfers by
recipient barangays.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to submit the required financial reports of the Local Government Unit as of December
31, 2016 and thereafter, to comply with the submission within the prescribed period
pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160, PD 1445 and COA Circular 95-006.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND where warranted during post-
audit of transaction documents which were submitted late.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of
accounts the grant of financial assistance to Non-government Organization as Due
from NGOs/POs and she should maintain subsidiary record to properly monitor the
liquidation of fund transfers.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
present the necessary documents as basis for the Time deposit account to determine
the authority and propriety of this placement.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer thresh out the differences of her
cashbook balance against the Cash-in-Bank subsidiary records to reconcile cashbook
balance with accounting records considering the materiality of discrepancy
amounting to P18,768,388.11.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer on the daily remittance by Collectors/Tellers to the liquidating officer of
their collections.

We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity or the Local Chief
Executive instruct the concerned officials of the municipality to strictly adhere to the
guidelines/rules and regulations under Republic Act 9184 and its Implementing Rules
and Regulations and COA Circular 2012-0001 in their procurement and payment of
goods and other claims.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND if warranted.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the relevant provisions of
DPWH DO No. 22, series of 2015 in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the
Contract.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of ND where appropriate.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials
especially the Municipal Engineer, BAC Members and the Municipal Accountant to
strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 and other provisions of
law.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND where appropriate.

We recommend that the OSCA and the MSWDO conduct the necessary
verification and home visitation in determining the final list of the Social Pensioners.
Further, immediately after due notification if the social pensioner did not appear in
the scheduled payout, the MSWDO should immediately conduct home visit for the
delisting and replacement and inform the Disbursing Officer that she/he can
immediately liquidate the unpaid cash advance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the officials concerned
to strictly adhere to the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to COA Circular No.
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of NS/ND if warranted.

We recommend that Local Chief Executive of LGU-Alangalang should give
priority on the implementation of its Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
officials/employees to maintain as current as possible and updated actual status of
work accomplishment of projects as of a given date.

We recommend that the management of Local Government of Alangalang
must formulate and adopt policies duly approved by concerned officials of the LGU
in the utilization of the donated Farm tractor that can fully benefit the intended
farmer beneficiaries of the Municipality.

We recommend that management provide a garage for the protection of the
farm tractor and accessories against fast deterioration of the equipment.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the accounts and financial reports of the Local Government Unit as of December 31, 2015 and thereafter, to comply with the submission within the prescribed period pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160, PD 1445 and COA Circular 95-006.
observations.reason: Although the LGU is submitting their accounts and reports, however, it is not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97 - 002 dated February 10, 1997, COA Circular No. 96 - 004 dated April 19, 1996 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 specifically on the following: 1.Stop the granting of government funds advances to officers and employees if their previous cash advances are not yet fully liquidated. 2.Require the officers and employees concerned to liquidate cash advances within thirty (30) days for local travels and sixty (60) if travel abroad after his return to his permanent official station as required under Section 16 of EO 248 as amended. Likewise, other cash advances must, as a rule, be liquidated within the prescribed periods depending upon the nature and purpose of the cash advance as stated in COA Circular No 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. 3.Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the head of agency or his duly authorized representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate his cash advance for travel and for other. 4.Suspend the salaries and delete the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time cash advances are fully liquidated. 5.Institute appropriate action for those who have no salaries that can be withheld.
